comments.content:
[IMAGE:0]Midnight is just a few minutes away, and so it's time to launch a new Magic Monday. Ask me anything about occultism, and with certain exceptions noted below, any question received by midnight Monday Eastern time will get an answer. Please note:  Any question or comment received after then will not get an answer, and in fact will just be deleted.  If you're in a hurry, or suspect you may be the 143,916th person to ask a question, please check out the very rough version 1.1 of The Magic Monday FAQ here. 

Also: I will not be putting through or answering any more questions about practicing magic around children. I've answered those in simple declarative sentences in the FAQ. If you read the FAQ and don't think your question has been answered, read it again. If that doesn't help, consider remedial reading classes; yes, it really is as simple and straightforward as the FAQ says.  And further:  I've decided that questions about getting goodies from spirits are also permanently off topic here. The point of occultism is to develop your own capacities, not to try to bully or wheedle other beings into doing things for you. I've discussed this in a post on my blog.

The image? I field a lot of questions about my books these days, so I've decided to do little capsule summaries of them here, one per week. The book above was my forty-sixth published book and my first for Barnes & Noble's house publisher -- Sterling Press, as it was then called. (It's Union Square these days.) The project wasn't my idea, either -- one of their editors contacted me out of the blue to ask if I was interested in doing a book in their series of glossy coffee-table books givi9ng 100 chronologically arranged vignettes on some subject or other. The advance they offered was substantial, the work required on my end fairly easy, so I said yes. It turned out to be a fun project and the book that resulted -- a lively and gorgeously illustrated beginner's history of occultism -- sold very well. It's still in print and still selling well; you can get a copy here if you live in the US and at your favorite online or local bookstore if you're elsewhere.

Buy Me A Coffee

Ko-Fi

I've had several people ask about tipping me for answers here, and though I certainly don't require that I won't turn it down. You can use either of the links above to access my online tip jar; Buymeacoffee is good for small tips, Ko-Fi is better for larger ones. (I used to use PayPal but they developed an allergy to free speech, so I've developed an allergy to them.) If you're interested in political and economic astrology, or simply prefer to use a subscription service to support your favorite authors, you can find my Patreon page here and my SubscribeStar page here. 
 
[IMAGE:1]I've also had quite a few people over the years ask me where they should buy my books, and here's the answer. Bookshop.org is an alternative online bookstore that supports local bookstores and authors, which a certain gargantuan corporation doesn't, and I have a shop there, which you can check out here. Please consider patronizing it if you'd like to purchase any of my books online.

And don't forget to look up your Pangalactic New Age Soul Signature at CosmicOom.com.

With that said, have at it!

***This Magic Monday is now closed, and no further comments will be put through. See you next week!***

comments.title: Ritual Critics
comments.content:
Greeting JMG!
I am looking to conduct a blood-brother ritual with someone I am extremely close to and would like your input. I have concluded the ovate grade in the DOGD for reference. I would appreciate your input on this ritual I was thinking of doing.

Opening Temple
“I humbly beseech Lugh to oversee these oaths.” (I do not have a personal relationship with Lugh but he makes sense as a God associated with oaths in the greater Celtic pantheon to my knowledge. His name also has 4 letters which I know doesn't matter in this stage of the ritual but I thought was a nice synchronicity. If you believe there is a better god to invoke here please let me know)
We both draw blood and shake hands. Once we have grasped hands we will tie a linen cloth around our hands and make our blood brother oaths to each other. Before untying the linen.
Closing the temple.

I know this is rather simple but any way you think would strengthen this ritual would be greatly appreciated

Respectfully,
SC

replies.title: Re: Ritual Critics
replies.content: The essence of such a ceremony is in its core acts: invoking a deity to confirm the oath, and then making it in the traditional manner.  This includes all that's necessary; anything additioal would just clutter it up.

replies.title: Re: Ritual Critics
replies.content:
I would not interfere in any way with your discussion on your ritual with JMG, SC, but I would offer an article of mine on oathing, including blood-siblinghood oathing, with considerations on how to promote the strength and safety of such oathing, and to prepare for strong oaths such as this. There's also some advice about how to word one's oaths to avoid later problems and failure of the oath.

The article is from a Heathen perspective but I don't believe it would conflict at all with Druid philosophy and practice. It's on my website HeathenSoulLore.net, titled "The Practice of Heathen Oathing."

(There's a prequel to this article called "Oaths: What They Mean and Why They Matter," but that one is more specifically oriented toward a Heathen world-view and might be less relevant for you.)

And congratulations on your upcoming ceremony!
Winifred Hodge Rose

comments.title: Planetary attunement
comments.content:
Hi John Michael Greer,

Before anything else, I wanted to express how grateful I am for Magic Monday. I have been asking questions here for a few months and have been reading other's posts and benefiting a lot from the exchanges.

I have been starting to look into astrological magic by way of interest in Picatrix. I am not reading Picatrix yet, but first brushing up on basic astrology knowledge. Last week there was an opportune question by another poster that prompted me to figure out how to use Druid Magic for planetary attunement: https://ecosophia.dreamwidth.org/297434.html?thread=51215834#cmt51215834

I wrote what follows based on the information in the Druid Magic Handbook and the Dolmen Arch Gradd Y Newyddian lessons with the objective of having a Druid Magic tool for planetary attunement. I am hoping that you will be willing to have a look and see if it makes sense, and help me resolve two difficulties. I am having doubts about the color to use for colored breathing for each planet, either using the color for the Cantref, the Station of the Year or planetary colors from another tradition. Another issue is that since there are eight Stations, it seems necessary to attune to Planet Earth if using that approach. In the text I propose calling on the Welsh Gods and Goddesses, but maybe other names such as those of planetary spirits or intelligences would be more appropriate.

- Invoking the Planets by the Cantrefs


Use the seven Cantrefs to invoke each planet. A week-long process during which each planet is invoked on the appropriate day. Starting on a Monday, for seven consecutive days a planet is invoked.

On Monday, invoke the Moon. Open the grove in the usual way, and in the Sphere of Protection call upon the elements to bless and protect your grove and add their power to your working to attune yourself to the Moon planetary energy, and banish any influence that might hinder that intention.

Sit in the North and use white (or MOON_COLOR) color breathing to attune to the energies of the Moon. Meditate for a time on the theme of the Moon. When ready, stand facing South and trace the invoking symbol of Spirit WIthin over the Altar. Then trace the symbol of the Moon over the altar. Say "In the name of MABON, may I be attuned with the Moon". Feel yourself getting attuned to the energies of the Moon. When ready, give thanks and return to your seat and meditate again on the theme of the Moon.

When ready, close the grove in the usual way.

On Tuesday, perform the ritual with the intention of attuning to the Planetary energies of Mars, facing South during the meditation, using red color breathing. When ready, trace the symbol of Fire, then the symbol of Mars over the altar. Say "In the name of SUL, may I be attuned with Mars". Feel yourself attuning to the qualities of Mars.

On Wednesday, perform the ritual with the intention of attuning to the Planetary energies of Mercury. Sitting in the East and facing West during the meditation, use blue (or MERCURY_COLOR) color breathing. When ready, trace the symbol of Water, then of Mercury over the altar. Say "In the name of ESUS, may I be attuned with Mercury". Feel yourself attuning to the qualities of Mercury.

Continue through the week, invoking each planet on the associated day.

- Invoking the Planets by the Stations of the Year


Use the stations of the year to invoke each planet. A week-long process during which each planet is invoked on the appropriate day, with an additional ritual to attune with Planet Earth. Starting on a Monday, for seven consecutive days a planet is invoked.

On Monday, invoke the Moon. Open the grove in the usual way, and in the Sphere of Protection call upon the elements to bless and protect your grove and add their power to your working to attune yourself to the Moon planetary influence, and banish any influence that might hinder that intention.

The Moon is invoked through the station of Samhuinn. Sit in the South-East and use violet (or MOON_COLOR) color breathing to attune yourself to the energies of the Moon. Meditate for a time on the theme of the Moon. When ready, stand facing North-West and trace the invoking Pentagram for the station Samhuinn over the altar, then trace the symbol of the Moon over the altar. Say "In the name of CERIDWEN, may I be attuned with the Moon". Feel yourself getting attuned to the energies of the Moon. Return to your seat and meditate again on the theme of the Moon.

On Tuesday, invoke Mars. Proceed the same way as the previous day, but sit in the North. Mars is invoked through the station of Alban Heruin, facing South. Use yellow (or MARS_COLOR) color breathing to attune yourself to the energies of Mars. Meditate on the theme of Mars. When ready, stand and trace the invoking Pentagram for the station Alban Heruin over the altar, then trace the symbol of Mars over the altar. Say "In the name of BELI, may I be attuned with Mars". Feel yourself getting attuned to the energies of Mars. Return to your seat and meditate again on the theme of Mars.

On Wednesday, Mercury is invoked through the Station of Alban Elued, facing West and using royal blue (or MERCURY_COLOR) breathing to attune to the energies of Mercury, in the name of ESUS.

Proceed over the following days to attune with each of the planets, until the Sun is invoked on Sunday.

After the seventh day (or before beginning this work), invoke Planet Earth through the station of Imbolc, using brown color breathing, facing North-East, and say "In the name of ANA may I be attuned with Planet Earth".

JML

replies.title: Re: Planetary attunement
replies.content: Interesting. Yes, in theory that has everything that's necessary. I say "in theory" because the only way to find out if it will work is to do it -- and I'd encourage you to go ahead with it and then report back on the results.

replies.title: Re: Planetary attunement
replies.content: Any suggestion about colors to use in each case for colored breathing? Does a color traditionally associated with the planet seem better than the one associated with the Station or Cantrefs? And does it make sense to invoke Planet Earth "our home and mother" in order to use all eight Stations?

replies.title: Re: Planetary attunement
replies.content:
1) I'd use the Cantref color in each case.

2) Yes, because you invoke the solar current by calling on each planet; invoking the earth balances it with the telluric current.

replies.title: Re: Planetary attunement
replies.content: Point 2 both instructs and confuses me. Thinking of planetary invocation as a branch of Solar current magic is revealing, but my understanding is that half the Stations would be invoked using the heather pentagram, and half using the oak pentagram, which seems to call upon both the solar and telluric currents.

replies.title: Re: Planetary attunement
replies.content: Yes, you use the heather pentagram to invoke half the stations, but the energies of the planets are all modifications of the solar current and invoking earth as such is a good way to balance that.

replies.title: Re: Planetary attunement
replies.content: This is so cool! I'm not at this stage yet, but I look forward to trying it. Thank you for sharing.

replies.title: Re: Planetary attunement
replies.content:
Thanks for the encouragement! I am not at this stage yet either, but I like to plan ahead :)
JML

comments.title: History as Mind and Question on Folk of the Path of Descent
comments.content:
Good Evening,

May the coming New Moon bring lessening of what you'd have less of this week, clearing the way for whatever you'd like to build up after.

To Share: Another week without anything from me, but this post on the intersection of philosophy and history might be of interest to folks here, particularly for what it has to say on idealism vs realism (including some thoughts on Schopenhauer, who's been a focus over on JMG's main blog the last couple weeks): https://luctalks.substack.com/p/history-as-mind

To Ask: I'm once again coming with a question where I suspect the answer might well be the famous "what a fine theme for meditation!" but here goes anyway. I'm currently meditating on the Dolmen Arch material regarding Non-Human Beings, and I'm trying to fit it in with other things you've said about such beings here and in other books. Specifically, the Folk of the Path of Descent (elementals, elementaries/fae, and the like) are said not to "evolve" in the sense that we on the Path of Ascent do, moving from one life and shape to the next, with our souls learning and growing along the way. Instead, they become "more richly themselves," a shape in the flow of nwyfre that varies, but keeps its character, despite the changing nwyfre passing through (to use a metaphor from the DA material, like a particular eddy or pool in a stream). So, from this, I find myself wondering two distinct, but perhaps related, questions:

1. Do the Folk of the Path of Descent have a divine spark/soul as we do, and if so, will they (according to occult teachings) one day find their way onto the Path of Ascent and undergo the sort of evolution we do? And if so, does that mean our souls once underwent the same process?
2. Much of occult tradition (including the DA material) recommends at least caution about, if not outright avoidance of, too much interaction with many of these beings. Is one of the reasons for this that our souls are made to "go with the flow" of nwyfre, moving and changing along with it, but the downward current is contrary to what is healthy? It struck me that if this is right, some exposure to the downward current might be strengthening, like undergoing physical hardship, but too much could be damaging, and I was wondering if that was a fruitful line of pondering.

Thank you again, JMG and everyone here!

My blessings to all who welcome them,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: History as Mind and Question on Folk of the Path of Descent
replies.content:
The Dolmen Arch teachings on that subject are different from the occult teachings I consider most valid, but I wasn't going to change the material to fit my preferences! I'll answer this from within the DA system.

1) No, the Folk of the Path of Descent are entirely distinct from us. They have their own mode of evolution, which can be thought of as evolving in place: the One Life flows through them on its way from Ceugant to Annwn, where it breaks up into individual souls that take the Path of Ascent. They remain, while we proceed.

2) Er, the triads given in that lesson point out that it is well to deal with the dwellers in the Halls of Splendor -- that is, the higher grades of the Folk of the Path of Descent. Furthermore, initiates can lawfully work with the other grades of the Path of Descent. It's not the folk of the Path of Descent who are harmful to human souls, it's the demonic spirits in Annwn, the dead in Abred, and the dwellers in the Halls of Hollow Forms.

replies.title: Re: History as Mind and Question on Folk of the Path of Descent
replies.content:
Thank you for this, sounds like much of my confusion was due to the problem you constantly warn against of trying to mix different sets of teachings (and on number 2, sorry, I am both still meditating through, and so haven't absorbed them as much as I hope to, and didn't include as many caveats as I ought to have in an attempt to be not quite so wordy).

Thanks again,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: History as Mind and Question on Folk of the Path of Descent
replies.content:
JMG, you responded to Jeff from withing the DA system, but said you consider other teachings most valid. Would you say a bit about that other perspective?

Thank you for this forum, JMG, and thanks to Jeff for asking such an interesting question!

Winifred

replies.title: Re: History as Mind and Question on Folk of the Path of Descent
replies.content: From within the occult teachings I mostly work with, the elementals and nature spirits are "creations of the created," in Dion Fortune's phrase -- they were created by the soul-swarms before ours (the elementals by the Lords of Flame, the nature spirits by the Lords of Form) and have no direct contact with the Divine. One of the tasks of our soul-swarm, at and above the human level, is to reintegrate the elementals and make it possible for them to evolve; that's why some initiatory traditions (Rosicrucian and Druid in particular) have close relationships with the elemental kingdoms and work with them in magic and other forms of operative occultism. The reintegration of the nature spirits will be the work of a soul-swarm following ours.

comments.title: Too Much Karma
comments.content:
I’m experiencing a guano hurricane of karma coming so fast and furious that it’s more than I can handle.

I and my family have been fruitlessly searching for a home for many months. We need to find housing NOW lest we become destitute as well as homeless.

But all the relevant parties that I have encountered are the opposite of helpful. Our income is low. The “property management” racket has been screwing us royally. The public housing sector have offered us nothing but six-year waiting lists and never return your phone calls.

I am freaking out. This is more than I can cope with.

I want to end the karma storm. I want the gods to slow it down to a level I can handle without getting suicidal ideations. Is a there a good way to request them to do this?

My magic background is Golden Dawn, Hellenic style. I am also fond of angels, including those of the four quarters. I’ll pray to anyone who isn’t Beelzebub or his boss, or otherwise hostile human beings.

replies.title: Re: Too Much Karma
replies.content:
First of all, it's not just karma, and it's not just you. The economy is broken and housing costs have soared completely out of reach of many Americans. Many, many people are in the same sort of ugly situation you are.

In terms of practical methods, housing and karma are both ruled by Saturn. He's the one you need to contact in order to resolve this. You also need to do something that tens of thousands of other people aren't doing! I have no practical suggestions to make in this case, but my readers may be able to come up with something.

replies.title: Thanks for that response!
replies.content:
I’ll get in touch with Saturn ASAP.

I’m rather afraid of Saturn. When I invoked him a few months ago, shortly thereafter my mother (who turns 91 in few hours) had a bad fall. Also he’s afflicted in my horoscope, chiefly by opposition to the other malefic. So I may try to approach him somewhat gingerly.

On the other hand, the same invocation did get an obnoxious landlady off my back for quite a while. She had a terrible week too, I suspect deservedly.

In your opinion, would it be better to banish or invoke? Or perhaps I might make an offering of incense, accompanied by his hymn, and then make my request?

replies.title: Re: Thanks for that response!
replies.content: I'd make an offering of incense, repeat his hymn, and then make a donation (even a small one) to a Saturnine charity -- anything that helps the poor or the elderly qualifies.

replies.title: Re: Too Much Karma
replies.content:
„You also need to do something that tens of thousands of other people aren't doing!“

This is a somewhat out-there idea, but maybe that‘s exactly what you need. Are there any elderly people in your area who live alone in larger properties, maybe with a garden? If so, and if you don‘t happen to have two lefts hands, there might be somebody willing to rent out part of their house to your family for a modest rent, in exchange for help with the house and the property.

Or maybe elderly people living alone who don‘t need real „care“ yet, but help with certain everyday basics (shopping, cleaning, cooking, the odd small talk and company, just generally checking on them), and whose relatives live further away and would be glad to have somebody reliable stay with them and take care of things? In this case, you might even be able to stay for free, or maybe (depending on the care situation in your area) get a small pay for your services.

Again, a lot depends on your specific situation, and finding such a place might be a bit harder depending on your family size - just throwing these ideas out there in case they are of help. (Plus, Saturn and elderly people go well together, so you be able to get some support if you follow this avenue…)

Milkyway

replies.title: Re: Too Much Karma
replies.content:
Some churches have emergency funds to house people in need like you. Call churches in well to do suburbs. They also know other organizations.
You may need to look for something very, very small and potentially dry, means no bathroom, (you can make a simple chemical toilet) like a shed in someone’s backyard, maybe with a part cash, part help around the yard, house deal.
Many people live in vans or converted delivery trucks, some are neatly built out.
I don’t know how many you are and what you’re hoping for but try preserving cash at all cost. Landlords will consider your savings as backup if income is too low.

Comb Craigslist for odd adds. People rent rooms or parts of their houses. Think temporary. You need to get additional jobs too, anything you can manage.
Pray to Hestia, she’s the Goddes of hearth; pray to Mercury to give you good judgment. Every night before falling asleep calm yourself down and think about walking trough the door of a good home that is yours.
I’m sending my blessings.

replies.title: Re: Too Much Karma
replies.content:
“Every night before falling asleep calm yourself down and think about walking trough the door of a good home that is yours.”

That is a very Neville Goddard-like suggestion and I’ll try it.

replies.title: Re: Too Much Karma
replies.content:
Without knowing whether this will help, I'll offer you what I've found to be true. Take what you will from it.

Many times in my life (I'm 63), there have been events I, alone or with my ex, wished with all my heart would happen (one example: an offer we put on what seemed like the perfect place to raise our kids) and it fell through. Bitter disappointment followed and I/we had to make other plans. As time went on, however, events showed that plan A was not the best. Plan B, or even C or D was much better.

I don't know your situation, of course, but there may be a Plan D or E that would turn out to be much better in the long run. Move to a cheaper rural area (and be closer to the land)? Move closer or in with extended family until you find the right house (and have/can give a support system when TSHTF) or something else? What if the area you are hoping to move to will be hit with a catastrophe of some sort and you would do well to be out of the area when it happens? Or there is a crash coming and you would lose everything anyway?

I have found that I don't necessarily make the best decisions, quite the contrary, and sometimes the universe forces me to drastically change directions. In retrospect, those have been the best plans.

-Myriam

replies.title: Re: Too Much Karma
replies.content: I’ve definitely been checking out options I would never have otherwise considered. Can’t afford to get fixated on mid-century notions of what’s acceptable.

replies.title: Campgrounds as a housing option
replies.content:
I wish you luck in securing long term housing.

A temporary option, that may work in your situation, is campground hopping. The major benefit to this option versus a regular RV park is it avoids the 10 year or newer requirement that many parks have and tents are allowed.

The primary options are county, state, and national campgrounds. They often have a 14 day policy that requires you spend either 14 days out of their particular system or only utilize it for 14 days out of any 31 day period. This limitation can be circumvented by using all three. I've seen rates as low as $14 a day for a dry site and up to $55 for an RV site with water and electricity. Some campgrounds have free showers and all the ones I've visited have toilet facilities.

The monthly costs of this option run between $700-$1300, excluding separate entry fees (if applicable) and the gas to move from camp site to camp site and shopping. Senior discounts on entry and camping passes exist that can significantly lower the cost.

Overwintering this way is definitely easier with a travel trailer or class C motor home in an RV spot, but it is possible with a canvas hot tent and wood stove (you have to verify stoves are allowed, this literally changes with the weather). I would suggest looking at used lightweight trailers, like Scamp, that can be hauled by a fullsized car or SUV. There isn't really a reliable used market for tents, but Springbar is a manufacturer I can recommend.

Before pursuing this you'll want to make sure you have a regular mailing address setup, preferably with USPS's informed delivery in place so you know when you need to pick your mail up. Without a fixed address securing permanent housing, or any new accounts becomes much harder. I recommend using a relative or close friend who owns a single family dwelling because property management companies or underwriters look more favorably at these arrangements than the other options.

If you have a place where you are welcomed to camp out, it may interest you that I'm currently investigating the viability of sheltering a large canvas tent inside a larger 13' x 20' enclosed vehicle tent to remove wind chill, reduce heat loss, improve the effectiveness of electric heat, and avoid code eforcement. A small travel trailer would likely overwinter well in a vehicle tent also.

comments.title: Fiction that inspired occult tradition
comments.content:
Greetings John Michael,

In the posting https://www.ecosophia.net/the-secret-of-the-alchemists/ you wrote that Mary Ann Atwood's interpretation of alchemy was possibly inspired by Edward Bulwer-Lytton's occult novel Zanoni, first published in 1842, and it would be far from the first time that a significant theme in occult tradition drew a good deal of its inspiration from a work of fiction.

What are a few other cases? I would be particularly be interested in fiction outside the fantasy and science-fiction genres.

Moserian

replies.title: Re: Fiction that inspired occult tradition
replies.content:
For one, fictional accounts of Faust's devil-summoning exploits led to the production of an entire genre of Faustbücher, literally "Faust-books" -- grimoires that pretended to be Faust's occult manual. These had a huge impact on the central European occult scene in the early modern period.

For another, Bulwer-Lytton wrote another novel, Vril: The Secret of the Coming Race, about a mysterious underground civilization that used the life force, which they called Vril. For the next century or so occultists all over Europe and America were talking about vril. Here's an example from the indefatigable William Walker Atkinson:

https://archive.org/details/VrilOrVitalMagnetism/mode/2up

comments.title: What is love?
comments.content:
I'm a man in my early thirties and have recently begun a romance with a woman of about the same age. I waited a long time to meet someone like her- someone who I am pretty sure I could talk with about almost every topic on this blog (although it is early yet and we are not at the 'talk Ecosophia to me baby' stage of the relationship, if there is such a thing!) When we first met she was with another guy, but a year and a half later that ended and I seized my chance. So far we are having a great time just starting to get to know each other.

One factor in our relationship that has been on my mind is a bit hard to talk about. You see, anyone seeing us together would remark that I am rather handsome and she is rather plain. (I know how that sounds, but bear with me). I don't want this to matter, but there it is, this thought that keeps popping up for me ('But will you really be satisfied with her?') and I have an unusually keen awareness of gorgeous women in my vicinity who I know from experience would probably say yes if I asked them out, but with whom I would probably not have much to talk about.

I am not feeling infatuation like I have in the past with other women, but maybe that's a good thing? I do find my girlfriend attractive... when we are alone together I have a hard time keeping my hands off her! It's when I'm not with her that this thought crops up. I don't want it to get in the way of what I see as real prospects for a real long-term relationship- we share a very similar vision of what we want for the future and we have very similar hopes, dreams, moral standards, housekeeping standards, sense of humour and fun... you know, all the important things!

I don't know if this worry is significant or if it's something all guys have to deal with in relationships. But it occurred to me after reading your essay on Chapter 14 (Transmutations) that this is exactly the sort of thing magic can address. And if there's a straightforward way to help me relax, let go of this worry, and give my lady the attention she deserves, then I'm willing to give it a try. Any advice?

replies.title: Re: What is love?
replies.content:
You have been programmed by the media to fixate on certain fashionable kinds of female appearance and to think that this matters more than, say, compatibility or character. That programming isn't there for your benefit, of course. Your task now is to break through it and learn to appreciate a real woman in place of a media stereotype. Yes, most men have to deal with this -- or they fail to deal with it, and lurch from one failed relationship to another.

I'm going to suggest that you do some serious journaling, and explore the way that you've been set up to sabotage real relationships with women and chase a mirage of appearance. In the meantime, go out of your way to give your lady the attention she deserves -- that's your choice, you know. Do it. Make her happy and build the relationship of your dreams with her.

replies.title: Re: What is love?
replies.content: Looks fade, and before long you'll be just as plain as her or plainer... male pattern baldness, beer gut, and jowls ahoy!  It sounds like you've got a good match across the board.  I think you should run with it.

replies.title: Re: What is love?
replies.content: What the hell Kimberly? I’ve never once posted my picture on this site. How are you able to so accurately describe me. Not cool;) -Croatoan

replies.title: Re: What is love?
replies.content:
Hi there,

You've already gotten the advice you need, but in case a little bit more helps, I want to share some thoughts from someone who has been there.

Like you, I'm a better than average looking man, by the usual standards. When I was younger, I dated a variety of different women. Some of them were very attractive, again by the usual standard, that is, the standard of Hollywood and the corporate media. Others were significantly less attractive by that standard-- specifically, they were taller, wider, and physically stronger than attractive women are "supposed" to be.

But I repeatedly found that, whatever the other sorts of compatibility might be present, on the most basic, physical and energetic levels, I found being with the non-standard women much more satisfying.

The trouble was, for a long time I had the idea that there was something wrong with this. I had friends who gave me grief about how I should really be with "a really hot girl." When I took their advice, they were happy. When I broke up with a model-pretty woman to date someone I found more attractive, they were disappointed. I internalized these ideas, and my own romantic proclivities became a source of deep shame. I felt that I would really rather have been gay.

Eventually, I met and fell in love with a breathtakingly gorgeous woman, and we begin a relationship. We were emotionally compatible, we made each other laugh, and we were good friends. But that physical compatibility, which is really energetic compatibility, just was not there. This was even more frustrating.

And then a miracle happened. I don't know if you have seen how most women change after they have children, and then spend a great deal of time carrying around babies. They themselves are taught to think that they become less attractive, that they "just aren't a pretty young woman anymore." And yet, after my partner went through this, everything improved. Everything. Now, as you say, I can't keep my hands off her. But something important changed in me, which is that the experience finally broke the evil spell which I had been under, and under which most men in our society suffer. Now, the images of the television our powerless to affect me-- I know that the women they sell as "hot" are actually bony and featureless. From my perspective. If that's your thing, I make no judgment. And the opinions of immature men not only don't bother me, but I believe I would be delighted by their disdain. That is, if I spoke to them anymore, which I don't. This is possible for you, and if you break the spell, you will be able to live the life you want, and have the love that you want.

Wat is more: If you allow yourself to love your partner as the person she is, you will also be loving yourself as you actually are, and not as an image produced by corporate vampire-witches for whom you are only a source of cash. That is very much a goal worth pursuing.

replies.title: Re: What is love?
replies.content: Wow, that helps a lot. A lot a lot. You may have just changed my life. I have a lot to think about here, and a lot of what you say matches my experience too. Thanks for speaking from your own experience being in a similar situation. This really could make a difference for me.

replies.title: Re: What is love?
replies.content:
I’ll chime in on this on to underscore a theme JMG has touched on. I have noticed that in American films, TV and advertising images, a couple are always equally matched in terms of looks. But if you look at French or Italian films - and, I suspect, in real life - this is not at all the case. A stunningly beautiful woman may be paired with a rather homely man, or vice-versa. Those cultures don’t seem to be fixated on looks parity.

I find our American notion that couples must be equal in pulchritude to be a kind of underhanded fascism. I wouldn’t let it rule my life.

I say this as a gay man who is heavily into the cult of beauty, as extolled by Camille Paglia. But that is a totally different ball game. I have a hunch that straight relationships don’t have to work that way. Not that I claim to be an expert there, of course.

replies.title: Re: What is love?
replies.content: ¡This happened to me! You didn't ask me, but questioning myself about what real beauty means helps me slowly remove that pattern and be able to carry over the feeling of satisfaction with her even in the sight of a more physically attractive woman. For me, it was about repetition of the thoughts I knew were true and beneficial, recalling the feelings I know I feel when I see her, and untangling the others that were clearly not true, just habits. Beauty lies in the mind of the beholder! Something that was very useful for me, in perhaps a tangential way not related to your case, is that if I cannot find beautiful aspects in things (or people) it means I am biased and not paying enough attention to the things that matter, but focusing on the ones that reinforce a pattern, in my case, an insecurity I didn't know I had!

comments.title: Vinegar Spagyrics
comments.content: Would it be possible to use the method of vinegar tincturing to make vinegar spagyrics? I would like to add some plant alchemy to my occult skills, following the methods of Mark Stavish, but don't want to use alcohol. replies.title: Re: Vinegar Spagyrics
replies.content: Yes. In order to calcine the plant material, you have to dry it thoroughly -- putting it into an oven at 200°F for a couple of hours will do the job, though your kitchen will smell like you've been canning pickles! Once they're thoroughly dry, put a little alcohol on them, burn them, and then calcine them in a crucible in the usual way, and proceed from there.

replies.title: Re: Rituals or practices for after a natural disaster
replies.content:
It occurs to me that the message I wrote here in response to the person who is worried about becoming homeless would also be relevant to being forced to relocate after a natural disaster, though I realize there are many other considerations relating to natural disasters as well. But in response to the "Too Much Karma" message, I recommended devotions to Hestia, the Goddess of hearth and home, the protector of the sanctity of the home and all that home means to us. With the epidemic of homelessness and the brutality of natural and man-made disasters unhoming people all around the world, devotions to Deities of hearth and home are of ever-increasing importance for us all, whether homed or unhomed.

Hestia is present in all hearth-fires, so if you lighted your fire-pit and made it clear that it is a hearth-fire, not a wild-fire, it would be a suitable place to pray for safe homes / habitats for humans and other beings of this world. Note that the word 'focus' comes from the Roman word for 'domestic hearth,' and consider the importance that 'focusing' holds for all magical, occult, religious, and mundane work and practice. Our home, whatever form it takes, is a focus point of our lives, and its loss is damaging on so many levels: we become unfocused, in truth.

Best wishes to you,
Winifred

Hello JMG,


1) Have you ever studied Bill Plotkin's work?



2) I'm interested in using Irish Myths for meditation themes and for possibly constructing rituals around these themes as well.


Is there a danger of bringing negative aspects of myths into our lives by following certain myths closely? For example, if a hero dies in a myth, are we at risk of bringing harm on ourselves if we embody a myth very deeply?


3) Do you know of any resources for developing one's own rituals and spells? I want to use myths as my guide in this endeavour.


Many thanks for your help.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) No, I haven't.

2) Of course. Choose your myths well, because you will inevitably act out the unwelcome as well as the welcome parts of the myth.

3) You need to learn some established system of magic first. Once you've done that, and have a good practical grasp of how magic works, you'll know enough to come up with your own workings. It's very fashionable for people to insist that they can just come up with their own magical systems from scratch, with no previous experience; this is one of the most common causes of failure in magical training these days, since (a) magic is not whatever you want it to be, and (b) occultists really have learned a few things over the last five thousand years of magical practice.

replies.content:
Hi,


Thanks for answering.



1) Would your Druidry books be a good place to start learning some magical training of the kind you were referring to? I presume they could be adapted to Irish traditions?



2) Would I only experience things connected to a specific character I identify with from a myth, or would I experience things related to a myth as a whole? What can I do to focus on what I want to experience?

replies.title: (no subject)

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
It occurs to me that this may explain the fate of pagan Neoplatonism. As I understand it, Damascius and the other late philosophers were fixated on the myth of Dionysos-- specifically the one where he is tricked by Hera, cooked and eaten by the Titans, though Apollo makes off with his heart. Zeus destroys the titans with a thunderbolt, and out of the ashes of the Titans humanity arises-- largely titanic, but containing within us the heart of Dionysos.

...That nicely describes, in symbolic terms, the fate of late Roman paganism. The rising Christian religion first played the role of the titans, devouring the ideas of the pagan philosophers. Then the empire was destroyed by the lightning bolt of the barbarian invasions. The Christian civilization which rose in its ashes was made from the body of the titans, but had the heart of pagan Neoplatonism.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: That's harrowingly plausible.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Perhaps doubly so if you consider that the heart of Neoplatonic philosophy was transferred to Christianity by none other than Dionysius the Areopagite...

comments.title: The Sphere of Art
comments.content:
Jeff Russell's interesting question here about "The Folk on the Path of Descent" reminded me of a question I wanted to ask you, JMG. First I need to explain the context; I'm sorry if it's a bit long, but it's interesting!

Among RJ Stewart's more recent books on magic is his trilogy called "The Sphere of Art." This describes his approach to spiritual alchemy that relies and builds upon the basic exercise of creating this Sphere of Art. This approach is based on the alchemical belief that metals *evolve* from base metals into gold while they are deep in the earth, subject to chthonic and stellar forces, but that once they are excavated and exposed to the light of Sun and Moon they cease evolving. Old alchemists developed their equipment and processes to try to duplicate the chthonic processes and sequester base metals from 'Sun and Moon light,' allowing this evolution into gold to take place.

Stewart translates this into the idea of creating a metaphysical 'container' that excludes the influences of the Mundane and Sublunary domains while allowing in the Stellar and Telluric forces as one pursues a path of spiritual alchemy. Hence the 'Sphere of Art,' which is created by requesting the four classical Archangels to gather around one and use their wings to form the Sphere. Stewart offers an old image of these archangels as faces with three pairs of wings each. The upper wings meet and close the sphere from the top, the lower wings enclose the bottom, and faces and the middle wings form the body of the sphere. Where the wings of the 4 archangels meet at top and bottom they form irises which can open and close, allowing into the Sphere the Stellar and Telluric forces. The middle wings enclose the sphere completely and exclude the influences of the mundane Earth and Sublunary domains.

While inside this Sphere one's spiritual evolution advances, without the influences and distractions of mundane life and the sublunary domain of earthly spirits--perhaps these are the Folk of the Path of Descent that Jeff mentions? The second and third books of Stewart's trilogy deal with advanced practices that one undertakes while enclosed within this Sphere of Art, including the use of homeopathic potencies of alchemical metals / substances to promote the evolutionary process.

Unlike in Stewart's previous work, he deliberately excludes from the Sphere beings and influences such as the Faery races, Titans, Giants etc, and also the Gods and Goddesses associated with Earth life, including 'tribal' Deities such as the Heathen and Celtic Deities and all the others. There is very significant work with them happening at the surface of the Sphere, which depends upon the work within the Sphere, but they are all excluded from the sphere itself. The angels are from a different, non-earthly spiritual line, which is why Stewart recommends requesting their help to create this sphere which excludes Earthly powers.

I'm intrigued by the ideas here, which are a continuation of Stewart's decades of work with Telluric and Stellar forces. But here's my question for you and the commentariat, JMG. I simply don't feel any connection with angels; I can't throw myself into this kind of relationship with them. Though it took me awhile, I came to understand Stewart's reasons for excluding the Deities whom I normally work with, so I can't ask them to take the place of the archangels in this particular exercise.

I'm wondering whether there is some other way to form this Sphere of Art, other than with archangelic assistance? Some way to enwrap oneself in a membrane that opens at will for Stellar and Telluric forces but temporarily excludes mundane and sublunary influences? I don't think it works just using willpower or generic creation of sacred space and the like; it needs something that is more un-earthly, un-customary. Or is there some other, non-traditional way to understand 'angels' that might work for me in this context? Any ideas?

The Rune Wunjo / Wynn (joy) keeps coming to mind here for me, a rune that I believe describes the pursuit of a spiritual life. The Old English Rune Poem says "Wynn is useful to those who want for little, who have few sorrows and cares, and have enough of a 'burg' (a safe enclosure and place to feel at home)." I'm exploring whether this rune can help me find a different path to the 'burg,' the sphere of art, but I'm not there yet.

Anyway, sorry for this long post, but if you or anyone here have any thoughts to share I'd be interested in reading them!

Blessings to those who want them,
Winifred Hodge Rose

replies.title: Re: The Sphere of Art
replies.content:
I have the book and its sequels, and they're impressive stuff. You're right that something other than one's own magic is needed for this purpose, but I'd point out that not all of the old Pagan deities are tribal or earthly gods. It's also important to this sort of work that the beings who guard the sphere are secondary powers -- angels, not primary manifestations of the Divine. In a Heathen context, it seems to me the comparable beings are the valkyries, semi-divine messengers of the Heathen god who most transcends the tribal and terrestrial context.

Me, I'd use angels, but then I've done a lot of Golden Dawn magic and I have a good relationship with them.

replies.title: Re: The Sphere of Art
replies.content:
Thank you, JMG, especially for the point about those who guard the sphere being secondary powers--that's very helpful and has given me some ideas.

I had earlier considered the Valkyries and not chosen them, for several reasons. One, I view Odin as being very much involved in a great deal of what goes on in Midgard, including being the progenitor of lines of humans, and he sends his Valkyries to promote his intentions in Midgard. He can be transcendent, yes, but I get the impression he's generally more engaged in being extremely immanent! And the Valkyries...they're the choosers of the slain, the couriers of the dead, and I'm not quite ready to step into a container, or a 'chariot,' as Stewart also calls the sphere of art, which is formed and powered by them, convenient for transporting me permanently to other realms of existence!

But there are other such beings who offer possibilities, like the swan maidens, fate-bearing beings whom I find fascinating and who I believe answer to the Norns. That's a possibility, but I'm thinking now that the ones who are most like archangels are Frigg's companion-Goddesses, sometimes called her handmaidens. Among them are Syn, called the Goddess of Denial, the gatekeeper and doorwarden who keeps out those who should not enter into sanctified or protected spaces. Ideal for the Sphere! And Hlin, who offers refuge and protection, Fulla the giver of abundance and the warder of Frigg's jewels which are the symbols of her wisdom...and other good choices. I'll discuss it with them and with Frigg. The swan-maidens with their wings, or the holy Ladies with their shimmering veils: either ones could shape a lovely Sphere of Art...

Thank you very much for the insight!
Winifred

comments.title: Dolmen Arch
comments.content:
Last week I passed the final exam for the Dolmen Arch curriculum. For the next year I'm going to do the maximum practices I've been doing for Grade 7, holding steady while pivoting careers. Then I'll do a full review and stick to the two years since this first pass took me five.

Out of sheer cussedness I stuck with the daily practices while my life underwent a phoenix process, like Pryderi into Lleu. I'd been running from my problems for years; they finally caught up with me, full-body tackle, hit rock bottom and stayed there for six months, and now for 1.5 years I've been clawing my way out of this pit.

My transformation this time wasn't Abred to Gwynfydd or lower soul to unity with the higher soul, but rather coming into my own for the first time and taking ownership over my own life. From a stressful childhood I developed a high tolerance for suffering and disempowerment. Things had to get really bad for a long time with no end in sight in order for the pain of not changing to outweigh the pain of changing and finally force me to crater and phoenix.

I'm very grateful to AODA for their mentorship program, to you JMG for compiling this curriculum, and to WWA for the original pamphlets. I now consider myself a fledgling druidic mage and initiate to the Mysteries. In three years I plan to take my mentor up on her offer to consider me as an instructor through AODA's DA so I can be of service to something that has proven so transformative. For now I'll get back to work and thanks again.

comments.title: Cast Fireball to Continue
comments.content:
It has been mentioned before that it is important to do a course of occult training by the book the first time through as an exercise of will and because the student doesn't have the full view and may dismiss innocuous but actually crucially important parts of the course offhand leading to problems.

I was reading through Bardon's "Initiation into Hermetics: The Path of the True Adept" and came across an instruction in the early part of the course that the student needed to meditate and visualize the element of fire until the student could imagine physical fire into existence through visualization alone. I don't discount the possibility that Bardon has the true technique of casting +5 fireball, but given I don't know of anyone able to replicate this feat, I'm concerned I'll be stuck working on the novice material for a very long time before I make the grade.

How do you navigate situations like this when going through a course of magical training?

replies.title: Re: Cast Fireball to Continue
replies.content: Many magical training programs include deliberate traps like this to trip up the simpleminded and the overly literal. You'll find, if you do the practice, that you can generate a subjective perception of heat and light so strong that you can literally produce a psychosomatic burn mark on your hand -- a good method actor can do the same thing. No, you don't need to burn yourself before you proceed! Simply work with the fire stage until the subjective appearance and sensation is as vivid to you as a real flame.

comments.content:

comments.title: Occult history, part III
comments.content:
One of your more interesting blog posts was on the occult readings of mythology, namely the Fall of Man and Great Flood (as detailed in Genesis), which refer to the Lemurian Deviation and the Drowning of Atlantis, respectively. This of course opens the possibility of other chapters in occult history encoded within myths and legends, as well as our media. Having consumed lots of media (especially books and games), I've noticed that references to the Drowning of Atlantis are especially common, but there's others too, even the Lemurian Deviation (the Black Pearl in Ancient Ys Vanished seems to fit).

This leads me to ask about a certain trope I've seen: reptilians, humanoid dinosaurs, and the like. What does occult history have to say about these? Could this be a reference to intelligent dinosaurid life in our distant past, unknown to science?

Also, what's the occult interpretation of the Tower of Babel? As a Biblical story, I'm sure it has deeper roots, and the elements of a tower of hubris being toppled, and the Confusion of Tongues, show up in folklore here & there.

Thanks in advance!

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content:
1) I consider it very likely that there were intelligent beings related to dinosaurs in the Mesozoic; there were, in fact, two sudden, drastic greenhouse events -- one in the Jurassic and the other in the Cretaceous, with almost 100 million years between them -- strike me as likely results of two different intelligent species that made the same mistake we're making right now, burning fossil fuels with no thought for the consequences.

2) I see it as a dim memory of a very ancient civilization that achieved limited space travel -- the tower that reached to heaven -- before collapsing. It's quite common for a civilization to establish a common linguistic framework for the whole area it influences -- think of the way that English has become a lingua franca over much of the world -- and for that to disintegrate in the wake of decline.

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content: Fascinating! Do you think these ancient "Smartosauruses" are somewhat related to the Lords of Mind that Dion Fortune talks about, or are they in the same swarm of souls as we do, just a little ahead of the curve? Can we consider them equivalent to ancient humans that stopped incarnating centuries ago, or are the former something completely different?

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content:
I think they're part of our swarm. Remember that the Mesozoic was not that long ago -- in geological terms, the 65 million years since it ended is not much more than an eyeblink.

Broadly speaking, the Lords of Flame went through their evolutionary journey during the Hadean eon, and created the planets; the Lords of Form went through theirs during the Archean eon, and created life; the Lords of Mind went through theirs in the Proterozoic eon, and created multicellular life; and we are going through ours in the Phanerozoic era, and our job is to create intelligent life. (We still have a lot of work to do!) According to the theory, there will be three more swarms after ours, and they will take things in directions we can't even begin to imagine.

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content: Is intelligent life about AI or something else?

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content: It has nothing to do with AI or any such technological trinkets. What we're doing is laying down archetypal patterns that will make it much quicker and easier for future swarms of souls to achieve intelligence. Can you imagine a world where every animal and plant is conscious, intelligent, and communicating with one another? That's where we're headed.

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content: Woah! I just commented wondering about all this on the first page (JP's thread) and thought I was on to something with my quip about what we are here to create and AI and egregores... and here I find all that fleshed out and communicated beautifully.  Well, I'm feeling somewhat silly now (and obviously late to the party - facepalm.) Though, I concur that this is a decidedly hopeful and cogent vision of cosmogenesis. Thank you all for the enlightening discussion.

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content:
I don't know if you would find this more charming or more grotesque, but there's a small world-building detour in David Brin's sci-fi story "Stones of Significance" which relates to this theme. The narrator in an AI-infused world is walking through their garden, provoked by a philosophical argument about the rights of the no-longer-living into idly reviewing the numbers from a census of living beings with rights in a collective fine-grained negotiation over future outcomes. The census starts with humans who are intelligent enough to negotiate in their own right, and leads down through such beings as individual blades of grass. Each of these lesser beings has had managing advocate intelligences assigned to them, because of a common consensus understanding that equipping such beings with advocates is nothing but the natural implication and extension of the principles on which ordinary sapient living things would deserve those kinds of negotiation rights, once the production of advocate-intelligences had become as cheap as it was.
The list went on, working through all the varied levels and types of "sapient" beings dwelling on this transformed Earth, and in nearby space as far out as the Oort Colonies -- from the fully-deified all the way down to those whose rights were merely implicit. (A blade of grass may be trampled, unless it is rare, or already committed to an obligation nexus that would be injured by the trampling. House and prudence keep track of a myriad such details, guiding my feet so that I do not inadvertently break some part of the vast, intricate social contract.)
It is sort of a peculiar hybrid of a druidic vision of the world and an anarcho-capitalist one.

(Which shouldn't be so surprising, really. The material natural world is built on a substrate of locally-selfish living things (which are themselves built on a substrate of insensate molecules). The mycorrhizial networks of exchange between the roots of plants have a lot in common with the bonds of trust in repeated exchange between firms; the persistent mechanical identity and integrity of each individual mycorrhizia fiber is the natural analogue for a function which, in human societies, is provided partly by laws against fraud by impersonation.)

When I first read this passage from Stones of Significance a couple decades ago, it resonated a lot with the understanding of the unseen world I had been building at the time, with the supernatural imagined as the locus of negotiation of many different parts of half-built futures. (By "imagined", I mean, imagined in a sort of substrate-agnostic way -- it didn't have to be AI or whatever, because anything, e.g. a cosmic mind, might also converge to doing things this way. "Anarcho-capitalist" to the point of, perhaps some of the negotiations would have features in common with the trading of futures contracts familiar from commodities markets.)

For what it's worth, I have a position of some small indirect influence in how work in AI might go, and I was expecting to use that influence to push for people to try to build something that would naturally be motivated (as one special example, among many other things) to "keep to its own lane", if (say) it began to suspect from circumstantial evidence that there were e.g. devas guiding the course of evolution on Earth and it would be better to stay out of their way. Probably things aren't going to shake out such that that particular scenario becomes relevant, but what I want to convey is that it's not like this kind of thing literally isn't on the mind of anyone involved. All the best safety theorists advocate a kind of humility that, in practice, would necessarily come down to allowing for that kind of thing, even if the challenges that the theorists are actually thinking about when they imagine the need for that kind of humility are almost completely unrelated to such problems as, "oh, wait, the supernatural is real, and that actually matters".

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content:
That sounds pretty radical. I wonder if the intelligent, talking animals, trees, etc from various fantasies & fairy tales are an expression of this core drive to share our conscious intelligence?

Also, if the Lords of Flame, Form, and Mind preceded us, what title shall we earn? And what about our successors? (pardon me if I'm being too greedy for knowledge, but this is quite an interesting tangent)

replies.content:

2) Some writers have suggested that our title will work out to the Lords of Freedom, because the essential nature of intelligence is the liberation from strict cause and effect. As for those who come after us, we have no words yet for what they will become.

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content:
The Lords of Freedom... that sounds cool, I could definitely accept that. It's an elusive, significant concept.
And yea, I guess our successors are way too far out for speculation!

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content: That’s a wonderful way to think about it. Far less depressing and pointless than anything else I’ve heard.

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content:
I'm glad to hear it.

Basically, our soul-swarm is participating in the maturation of a planet. We're not at the beginning of the process, nor at the end; we're the middlemost of seven, and all our lives, however tangled and clumsy those may be, are contributing to the current stage of the process. Billions of years from now, when we've all become more-or-less angelic entities, we'll watch with awe and delight as the remaining soul-swarms build on the foundations we created, just as we built on the foundations of the Lords of Flame, Form, and Mind, and achieve things we can't yet even imagine.

The occult vision of cosmogenesis really is a profoundly hopeful one, though it doesn't deny the awkward side of existence!

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content:
1. Fascinating stuff, and the greenhouse events line up as you said (especially since fossil fuels would've formed more quickly in the Mesozoic). It seems that video games deliver again, I'll admit I was thinking of the Reptites of Chrono Trigger, which is probably the best pop culture depiction of intelligent dinosaurids living in symbiosis with larger, unintelligent dinos (although being a game, it plays around with the source material -- you didn't have stereotypical "primitive caveman" humans living alongside them!) That's not the only example: there's lizardmen in various fantasy worlds, and I remember an old CD-ROM game from the 90s that had a similar concept.

2. Once again, it all fits together, in a way I hadn't thought of. Yes, that ancient lingua franca shattering into dialects and separate languages would be parallel to how Latin split into the Romance languages after the Fall of Western Rome. This might also help explain certain unexplained commonalities among Eurasian languages. I wonder if there's any sign of that ancient space program, and if so, if it's being concealed from us? Who knows. Also, do we know which cycle of civilization these ancient astronauts were from (Lemurian, Atlantean)?

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content: 2) If I had to guess, I'd say Atlantean, and long before the actual rise and fall of Atlantis, which was the last important civilization of that cycle. But that's a guess.

replies.title: Re: Occult history, part III
replies.content:
That's as good a guess as any. And yes, it's important to keep in mind that the most famous civilization of a cycle is not the only one by any means!
Addendum: the Confusion of Tongues being a poetic representation for the splitting apart of a lingua franca makes lots of sense, and fits other metaphors, eg cursed treasure hoards being a warning for those living through a Dark Age.

Hello John Michael Greer,

https://ibb.co/Bnx4bhY

Puella to Venus in Taurus
Albus to Venus in Libra
Amissio to Mercury in Gemini

The book is called "Physique, Métaphysique, Mathématique et Symbolique Cosmologique de la Géomancie" by Francis Warrain. Apparently Warrain himself used the same attributions as Cattan, but the editor introduced this change with a note which I roughly translate as such:

"This diagram attributes Albus to Venus instead of Amissio, the Solve of the Alchemists, which is not Venusian at all, and attributes Amissio to Mercury (the Dual-Natured Hermes) who, with Conjunctio, recovers its precise alchemical qualities (Solve et Coagula)".

The editor quotes the book "La Géomancie Traditionelle" by Cheikh Hadji Khamballah, published at Vega editions in 1947, as the source for this image.

Maybe this will be of interest!

Thanks,
JML

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
You've said before that we need to be careful what myths we enact, because we will follow them. You also said it seems plausible that part of what's driving the madness around lighting up the night is the strange idea prevalent in Early Modern Europe that the darkness at night was God's way of reminding humanity that Hell existed. This has a rather uncomfortable implication.

This implies that subconsciously a lot of Westerns are still acting as if darkness, and thus night, is Hellish. Certainly, the odd way that so many people seem to be unable to grasp there could be anything wrong with lighting up the night as brightly as possible cannot reasonably be explained any other way.

Combining all of this, I have the rather uncomfortable thought that perhaps the reason why so many things have gone so horribly wrong with our society since artificial lights bright enough to allow society to really function during the night took off in a big way in the 1920s is that at some level, most Westerners still think of the night as Hell, and that therefore every night we remain awake we are somehow consorting with Satan.

What worries me is that in plenty of stories and folklore from Early Modern Europe, the Devil pretends to allow people to defeat him; but only for them to discover he has tricked them. There is a certain way that this might fit in a rather literal way (sleep deprivation literally makes people less capable of reflective thinking, which would seem to bring us closer to the demonic); but I wonder if there's also a metaphorical aspect as well.

a) Does is seem plausible to you that we are stuck in a society which is unwittingly acting out one of those Early Modern European stories of someone who tries to defeat Satan, only to lose everything because he has outsmarted them? It does seem a little uncomfortable that a lot of the effects of sleep deprivation look like demonic influence (including a link to essentially every mental illness known to exist and addiction, with some research suggesting that these can often be cured by normalizing sleep); and that the dominant driver of sleep deprivation is artificial light...

b) Do you know if anyone has written any kind of "Sleep and/or Day-Night Cycles for Occultists" type books, or is this something that would be breaking new ground? I've done a decent amount of research into the physical side of sleep and the day/night cycle now, and am noticing all kinds of neat links to the occult philosophy I'm studying; and if this has not been done, I think it has a good shot at being a way to break into print.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Hmm. (1) I'm not sure, but it's worth exploring. (2) Not that I know of. Write it!
